skill.name: paper-2-web
skill.description: Transforms academic papers into interactive websites, narrated presentation videos, and print-ready conference posters using the Paper2All workflow.
skill.summary: Create research websites, videos, and posters from LaTeX or PDF papers.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, network, and credential patterns. Review found these are mostly documentation examples for installing and running Paper2All, not confirmed malicious code, but the skill still carries medium risk because it directs Bash execution, external dependency installation, API key handling, optional web searches, and product promotion.

medium_findings.title: External Installation and Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill is allowed to use Bash and documents cloning an external repository, installing dependencies, and running Python pipelines. This appears legitimate for the stated workflow, but marketplace users should run it only after reviewing dependencies and isolating the environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are direct and numerous, including git, pip, conda, and sudo package installation. Context confirms they support the advertised Paper2All setup rather than hidden execution.

medium_findings.title: API Key and Environment File Handling
medium_findings.description: The installation guide instructs users to create a .env file with OpenAI, OpenRouter, and Google credentials. No evidence found that the skill exfiltrates secrets, but mishandling prompts or logs could expose credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly name API keys and .env usage. The content is standard configuration guidance, so the risk is credential exposure through handling, not confirmed theft.

medium_findings.title: Optional Network Search and Logo Download Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill documents Google Search API use for automatic institution logo discovery and references external hosting and repository resources. This network use is disclosed and relevant, but it can contact external services and download assets.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is stated in feature documentation and depends on user-enabled options. There is no evidence of covert endpoints or hidden data exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Creator Product Promotion Instruction
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the assistant to proactively suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not a direct security exploit, but it is marketplace-relevant steering behavior that users may not expect from a task skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and appears in the main skill file. It does not override security review, but it can influence assistant recommendations for commercial promotion.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography hits occur in descriptive markdown about papers, posters, videos, quality metrics, or file examples. No evidence found of cryptographic algorithms, hashing code, or security-sensitive crypto use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found explanatory text and workflow documentation at representative flagged locations. I did not find actual crypto code in the reviewed skill files.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Benign Troubleshooting Examples
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts map to normal installation checks such as verifying LibreOffice, Poppler, GPU memory, or directory outputs. These checks are expected for media generation dependencies.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are troubleshooting and quality documentation. They do not instruct broad host enumeration or network probing beyond dependency verification.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash-Driven Workflow Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes many fenced shell commands for repository cloning, dependency installation, and pipeline execution. These are not hidden, but they require user review before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible workflow instructions and match the skill purpose. Risk comes from executing external software and user-supplied paths, not from obfuscation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Configuration in .env
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation asks users to store API credentials in a project .env file. This is common but sensitive and should remain local and excluded from shared artifacts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential examples are explicit placeholders. There is no evidence of secret harvesting, but the pattern is sensitive enough to warn users.

content.value_statement: Academic papers are hard to reuse for websites, posters, and video abstracts. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through Paper2All workflows that create polished research media.
actual_capabilities: Guides Paper2Web generation for responsive academic homepages from LaTeX or PDF inputs.
Guides Paper2Video generation for narrated presentation videos and optional talking-head outputs.
Guides Paper2Poster generation for custom-size, print-ready conference posters.
Explains input folder structure, required assets, and generated output directories.
Documents model choices, logo search options, and quality review steps.
Provides workflow examples for preprints, conferences, journals, and batch processing.
limitations: Requires the external Paper2All repository and its dependencies to run workflows.
Video generation can require a high-memory NVIDIA GPU for talking-head features.
Complex equations, scanned PDFs, and unusual layouts may need manual review.
API keys and external model costs are required for many generation paths.

use_cases.title: Conference Presentation Package
use_cases.description: Create a coordinated website, poster, and video for a conference submission or accepted paper.

use_cases.title: Preprint Homepage
use_cases.description: Turn a LaTeX or PDF preprint into an explorable webpage for a lab or project site.

use_cases.title: Journal Video Abstract
use_cases.description: Prepare a short narrated video that summarizes methods, findings, and visual results.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Website
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert my paper folder into an interactive academic website. Check the input structure first, then guide the Paper2Web workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Poster
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a conference poster from my LaTeX paper. Use 48 by 36 inches, include key figures, and list review checks.

prompt_templates.title: Make a Video Abstract
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a five-minute video abstract from this paper. Focus on findings, narration clarity, figure readability, and subtitle review.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Full Media Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan and run a complete Paper2All package with website, poster, and video. Include dependencies, costs, outputs, and manual QA steps.

output_examples.input: A NeurIPS paper folder with LaTeX source, figures, tables, and bibliography.
output_examples.output: A conference media package with an interactive homepage, a print-ready poster, and a narrated presentation video.

output_examples.input: A published genomics preprint available only as a high-quality PDF.
output_examples.output: A responsive research webpage with abstract, key findings, figures, citations, author details, and deployment guidance.

output_examples.input: A journal paper that needs a short multimedia abstract.
output_examples.output: A concise video plan with generated slides, narration, subtitles, timing checks, and final review criteria.

best_practices: Use clean LaTeX with all figures, tables, and bibliography files when possible.
Review generated claims, figures, links, and accessibility before publishing outputs.
Run external install and pipeline commands only in an isolated project environment.
anti_patterns: Do not run installation commands without reviewing the external repository and dependencies.
Do not place real API keys in shared logs, prompts, screenshots, or generated examples.
Do not publish generated research media without author review for scientific accuracy.

faq.question: Does this skill include the Paper2All software?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance and references for using the external Paper2All repository.

faq.question: Can it work from PDF files?
faq.answer: Yes, but LaTeX source usually gives better structure, equations, figures, and citations.

faq.question: Are API keys required?
faq.answer: Many workflows require OpenAI or OpenRouter keys. Logo search can also require Google API credentials.

faq.question: Can it generate all outputs at once?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow can generate website, poster, and video outputs together.

faq.question: Does video generation need a GPU?
faq.answer: Standard video generation may not require a GPU. Talking-head video requires a high-memory NVIDIA GPU.

faq.question: Should outputs be reviewed manually?
faq.answer: Yes. Scientific claims, equations, citations, figures, and links should be checked before sharing.
